articles.title: "القوميون الإنجيليون في إدارة ترامب يدمرون الجانب الأخلاقي في النظام العالمي" – مقال رأي في الغارديان
articles.summary: "بالنسبة لمعظم المسيحيين الملتزمين، فإن استغلال الدين وتشويهه وتوظيفه كسلاح لتبرير الموت والدمار، وقصف إيران "لإعادتها إلى العصور الحجرية" أمر محزن للغاية. فالمسيحيون يؤمنون بأن يسوع صُلب من أجل البشرية جمعاء، من أجل غفران الخطايا، لا من أجل الانتقام والكبرياء والهيمنة".
articles.context: تتزامن هذه المقالة مع تصاعد التوترات بين الولايات المتحدة وإيران، خاصة بعد انسحاب إدارة ترامب من الاتفاق النووي الإيراني في مايو 2018. هذا القرار أدى إلى تجديد العقوبات على إيران، مما زاد من حدة الصراع في المنطقة. في السودان، حيث يواجه المسيحيون تحديات كبيرة، يُعتبر استغلال الدين من قبل القوميين الإنجيليين في السياسة الأمريكية تهديدًا للأخلاق العالمية ويؤثر على الديناميات الإقليمية.

articles.title: Ethiopian lawyer Meron Aragaw named to Courtroom Mail 100
articles.summary: Meron Aragaw Erkihun, former Executive Director of the Ethiopian Women Lawyers Association (EWLA), has been recognized in the 2026 Courtroom Mail 100 list of top legal professionals, highlighting her contributions to women’s rights advocacy and human rights in Ethiopia. During her tenure at EWLA from 2017, Meron led efforts to promote and protect women’s economic, […]
articles.context: Meron Aragaw's recognition comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing challenges related to women's rights and human rights, particularly following the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The conflict exacerbated gender-based violence and highlighted the need for strong advocacy. Meron's leadership at the Ethiopian Women Lawyers Association has been crucial in addressing these issues, especially as the Ethiopian government seeks to stabilize the country and promote gender equality in the post-conflict recovery phase.

articles.title: UNICEF says Iran war means more children will suffer in Somalia
articles.summary: The sound of a crying child is a sign of hope in a crowded displacement camp in southern Somalia — the most malnourished children are too weak to even cry. For the mothers in the Ladan camp in the town of Dollow, survival is the only thing on their minds — not the Iran war […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Somalia has been exacerbated by the 2022 drought, which has left millions facing severe food insecurity. The Iran war, particularly its implications for regional stability and resource allocation, diverts international attention and aid away from Somalia, where humanitarian needs are critical. The situation is further complicated by the presence of militant groups like Al-Shabaab, which continue to undermine efforts for peace and stability in the region.

articles.title: Death toll from Kenyan floods rises to 108, police say
articles.summary: Weeks of heavy rains and flooding have ​now killed 108 people ‌in Kenya, the National Police Service said on Saturday ​in an updated ​death toll. Flash floods, which began ⁠on the evening ​of March 6, have caused ​widespread destruction in the East African nation, sweeping away dozens ​of vehicles, disrupting ​air travel, and damaging power […]
articles.context: Kenya has faced recurrent flooding due to climate change and deforestation, with the 2020-2021 season witnessing significant rainfall that displaced thousands. The current floods, which began on March 6, 2023, exacerbate existing vulnerabilities, particularly in regions like Tana River and Garissa, where communities are still recovering from past climate-related disasters. This disaster highlights the urgent need for improved disaster management and infrastructure resilience in the face of increasing climate challenges.

articles.title: Rural Corridor Dev’t Set to Lift Living Standards of Nonurban Population in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed said Ethiopia’s rural corridor development initiative, which is an extension of the broader concept of city corridor development, aims to bring similar improvements in the living standards of rural communities. The PM said that “just as we are working to improve the livelihoods of our citizens in urban areas and make […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant challenges in recent years, including the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020, which exacerbated economic hardships and disrupted rural livelihoods. The government’s focus on rural corridor development comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize the nation post-conflict and address the disparities between urban and rural areas. This initiative aligns with the 2021 National Development Plan, which emphasizes inclusive growth and aims to rebuild trust among communities affected by the conflict.

articles.title: ElectioBoard Extends Voter Registration Deadline Amid Logistical Challenges
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia has extended the voter registration deadline to April 22, 2026, citing delays caused by online system disruptions, weak network coverage, and transportation constraints. The voter registration period for the 7th national election was initially scheduled to close on April 6, 2026, but the Board said the extension was necessary […]
articles.context: The extension of the voter registration deadline comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing political tensions following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has strained the electoral process. The National Election Board's decision reflects concerns over ensuring a fair and inclusive election, especially after the postponement of the 2021 elections due to security issues. The upcoming 7th national election is crucial for stabilizing the country, as it aims to restore public trust in the democratic process.

articles.title: At least 9 al-Shabaab terrorists killed during intelligence operation in Somalia
articles.summary: During operation in Lower Shabelle region, 6 heavy engines used by al-Qaeda-affiliated terrorist group to manufacture explosives also destroyed, says Intelligence and Security Agency
articles.context: The operation in Lower Shabelle comes amid heightened military efforts by the Somali National Army (SNA) and African Union Transition Mission in Somalia (ATMIS) to combat al-Shabaab, particularly following the group's recent attacks on civilian and military targets. The Somali government, under President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud, has intensified its offensive since the launch of "Operation Dhuul" in August 2022, aiming to dismantle al-Shabaab’s operational capabilities and restore stability in the region.

articles.title: Asymmetric Conflict in Somalia, Sudan: How it Affects Ethiopia
articles.summary: This week, Somalia’s national army announced it has taken control of the city of Baidowa in the country’s South West State. Located just 150 miles north-west of the capital Mogadishu, Baidowa is a strategic city that has served as a base for international peacekeepers, including Ethiopian troops, and humanitarian agencies in an area affected by […]
articles.context: Baidowa's capture is significant as it underscores the ongoing struggle between Somalia's federal government and the militant group Al-Shabaab, which has long threatened stability in the region. The Ethiopian National Defense Force (ENDF) has historically been involved in peacekeeping efforts in Somalia, particularly following the 2012 agreement between the African Union and Somali authorities to combat terrorism. The recent developments may impact Ethiopia's security posture and its role in regional stability amid the ongoing conflict in Sudan.

articles.title: Justice Ministry Seeks ETB 9.5 bln to Back Five-Year Reforms
articles.summary: Conflict, economic crime, corruption cited as major bottlenecks Officials at the Ministry of Justice say they require close to 10 billion Birr to implement an overhaul of the legal system before the decade is out. A new five-year plan issued by the Ministry seeks 9.5 billion Birr, one billion of which officials hope to source […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflict since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, leading to widespread human rights abuses and a breakdown of legal institutions. The ongoing tensions between the federal government and regional forces, particularly in Tigray and Oromia, have exacerbated issues of corruption and economic crime. The proposed 9.5 billion Birr reform plan is critical for restoring public trust and stabilizing the legal framework amid these challenges.

articles.title: Double Taxation, Contraband Eat into Khat, Oilseed Export Earnings
articles.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration reports that contraband trade and taxes levied by regional administrations have cut into earnings from the export of khat and oilseeds. Reporting to Parliament this week, officials told lawmakers that despite a record USD 6.7 billion in export revenues over the first eight months of the financial year, […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's khat and oilseed exports have historically been vital to its economy, especially amid ongoing conflicts in the Tigray region that have strained resources and trade routes. The federal government's recent push for regional administrations to streamline taxation comes after the 2020 Tigray conflict, which disrupted agricultural production and trade. As the country seeks to stabilize its economy post-conflict, the impact of double taxation and contraband on export earnings has become a pressing concern for lawmakers.

articles.title: Ethiopia Secures Hague Victory in Lease Arbitration Case
articles.summary: The Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia has secured a decisive victory in a 334 million euro investment arbitration brought by African Asset Finance Company Holdings B.V., a Netherlands-incorporated entity wholly owned by a Delaware-based parent company. This week, a tribunal constituted under the UN Commission on International Trade Law (UNCITRAL) dismissed all claims tied to […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's victory in this arbitration case comes amid ongoing tensions related to foreign investments in the country, particularly in the context of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam dispute with downstream countries like Egypt and Sudan. The ruling, delivered on October 10, 2023, is significant as it reinforces Ethiopia's legal position against foreign claims, reflecting its broader strategy to assert sovereignty over its resources while navigating complex international relations and economic challenges.

articles.title: Fuel Crisis Exposes Ethiopia’s Energy Fragility
articles.summary: Ethiopia is grappling with a fuel shortage that has rippled through every corner of the economy, a crisis triggered by the U.S.-Israel war with Iran and the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The blockade has choked off a significant portion of global oil flows, and for Ethiopia—entirely dependent on imports—the consequences have […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's reliance on imported fuel has intensified amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel tensions with Iran, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in October 2023. The disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has exacerbated the situation, as Ethiopia's energy sector struggles to cope with rising demand and limited supply. This crisis comes on the heels of the Ethiopian government's efforts to stabilize its economy post-conflict, particularly after the Tigray War, which ended in November 2022.

articles.title: Ethiopia Faces Multi-Crisis Stress Test as Overlapping Shocks Threaten Stability
articles.summary: Policy analysis urges shift from reactive crisis response to long-term resilience planning A policy analysis published this week by the Institute of Foreign Affairs (IFA) warns that Ethiopia is undergoing a “multi-disruption stress test,” as overlapping economic, environmental and security challenges place mounting pressure on the country’s governance systems. The study, authored by Amran Gamachu […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, yet tensions remain high in regions like Oromia and Amhara. The country faces severe economic challenges, exacerbated by the global impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and the ongoing effects of climate change, including droughts and locust invasions. These factors, combined with political instability, threaten the fragile governance structures established post-conflict.

articles.title: Agriculture Ministry Acknowledges Fertilizer Challenges as Crop Cultivation Nears Target
articles.summary: The Ministry of Agriculture has acknowledged ongoing challenges in fertilizer supply, even as its officials laud progress in productivity. Speaking at a press briefing on April 2, 2026, State Minister for Agriculture and Horticulture Development, Meles Mekonnen (PhD), said fertilizer provision has faced sustained pressure since the COVID-19 pandemic, compounded by global conflicts, including the […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's agricultural sector has been severely impacted by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, disrupting supply chains and access to essential inputs like fertilizer. The ongoing fallout from the COVID-19 pandemic further exacerbated these challenges, as global supply chains remain strained. The government's efforts to boost productivity are critical, especially as it aims to achieve food security and economic stability amid these compounding crises.

articles.title: Ethiopia Utilizing only Half its Export Potential: Afrexim Bank Report
articles.summary: A new report from Afrexim Bank indicates that Ethiopia has a long way to go to reach its full export potential, suggesting sustained growth levels of 12.2 percent annually could see the country earn nearly USD 20 billion by 2030. The report highlights that only a handful of African countries utilize more than 60 percent […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's export challenges are compounded by the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022, disrupting trade and economic activities. The government is now focused on economic recovery and diversification, particularly in light of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement, which came into effect in January 2021, aiming to enhance intra-African trade. This report from Afrexim Bank underscores the urgency for Ethiopia to capitalize on its agricultural and manufacturing sectors.

articles.title: Fertilizer Supply Concerns Headline AU Report on Iran War’s Consequences for Africa
articles.summary: A new report warns that disruptions to fertilizer supply as a result of the war in Iran could prove more consequential for African countries than the fuel shock currently facing the continent and the world at large. The report, published by the AU, African Development Bank (AfDB), and the UN, warns that disruptions to Gulf […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Iran, which escalated with the U.S. withdrawal from the nuclear deal in 2018 and subsequent sanctions, has disrupted global supply chains, particularly in the fertilizer sector. As Ethiopia grapples with food insecurity exacerbated by the Tigray conflict and climate challenges, the potential for reduced fertilizer availability threatens agricultural productivity. This situation is critical as Ethiopia relies heavily on imports for fertilizers, making it vulnerable to geopolitical tensions in the Gulf region.

articles.title: Hormuz Traffic Collapses by 95pct, Triggering Global Oil Shock: UN Trade Agency
articles.summary: Fuel retail prices jump in response to crisis Ethiopia is facing mounting economic pressure as a global energy shock triggered by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz drives up fuel prices, threatens inflation, and strains the country’s already fragile external balance. A report released on April 1, 2026 by the United Nations Conference on Trade […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended in November 2022 but has left deep economic scars and a fragile political landscape. The ongoing tensions in the Horn of Africa, particularly with the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam dispute involving Sudan and Egypt, further complicate Ethiopia's economic recovery. The recent collapse of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz exacerbates these challenges, significantly impacting Ethiopia's energy costs and inflation rates.

articles.title: Eritrea’s Crimes in Tigray: pathways to justice and peace
articles.summary: The prospects of accountability are currently slim, but non-prosecutorial avenues could help balance the quest for justice and peace. The Horn of Africa appears once again on the brink of renewed conflict. The situation has moved beyond shifting alliances, with Ethiopia and Eritrea reportedly deploying armed forces closer to the border. Against this backdrop of […]
articles.context: The 2018 peace agreement between Ethiopia and Eritrea marked a significant thaw in relations after two decades of conflict, yet tensions have resurfaced as Eritrean troops are implicated in the ongoing violence in Tigray since the civil war erupted in November 2020. The Ethiopian government's struggle to maintain control and the involvement of regional actors, such as the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), complicate the landscape for justice and accountability for atrocities committed during the conflict.

articles.title: Taking action for Africa’s agrifood systems amid global uncertainty
articles.summary: A call for practical, collective leadership on food security and nutrition Global shocks keep tightening their grip on Africa’s agrifood systems. Trade disruptions raise the cost of fertilizer, fuel and freight. Climate variability wipes out harvests. Conflict displaces farmers from their land. These are real, and they are intensifying. But the most consequential decisions about […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's agrifood systems are under severe strain due to the ongoing Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, displacing millions and disrupting agricultural production. Additionally, the 2022 drought exacerbated food insecurity, with the United Nations estimating that over 20 million people face acute hunger. The recent African Union-led ceasefire agreement in November 2022 offers a glimmer of hope, but the lingering effects of conflict and climate change continue to threaten food security in the region.

articles.title: The Missing Link in Ethiopia’s Financial Reform Agenda
articles.summary: Ethiopia has embarked on an ambitious financial reform journey. From public financial management modernization to tax system reforms and the gradual opening of capital markets, the country has signaled a clear intent to build a more transparent, efficient, and market-oriented economy. Yet amid policy redesigns, institutional restructuring, and regulatory upgrades, one critical element remains underemphasized: […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's financial reform agenda comes in the wake of the 2018 political transition led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, which aimed to liberalize the economy after decades of state control. The Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, has strained the country's resources and complicated these reforms. Additionally, the 2021 signing of the Pretoria Agreement aimed to establish peace, making the need for robust financial systems more urgent to support post-conflict recovery and stability.

articles.title: Italy’s football chief resigns after World Cup disaster
articles.summary: contThe head of Italy’s football federation (FIGC) resigned on Thursday, falling on his sword after the men’s national team failed to qualify for the World Cup for a third consecutive time. Gabriele Gravina revealed he would step down as the country’s top football official following a meeting held at the FIGC’s headquarters in Rome on […]
articles.context: Italy's failure to qualify for the World Cup is particularly significant as it highlights a broader decline in the country's footballing prowess, which has been a source of national pride. This resignation comes amid rising tensions within the Italian football community, exacerbated by the recent success of rival nations and the ongoing debate over governance and reform within the FIGC, following Italy's last World Cup win in 2006.

articles.title: Wingu Africa Launches Wingu Cloud Exchange in Ethiopia
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Wingu Africa, a major carrier-neutral, Tier III-standard data centre operator, announced the launch of the Wingu Cloud Exchange (WCX), a new private cloud platform tailored
articles.context: The launch of Wingu Cloud Exchange comes at a critical time as Ethiopia seeks to enhance its digital infrastructure amid ongoing political tensions and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that officially ended in November 2022. The Ethiopian government has been actively promoting digital transformation to attract foreign investment and improve economic stability, making Wingu Africa's initiative significant for fostering technological growth and regional connectivity in a recovering nation.

articles.title: NEBE Warns Against Coercion in Voter Registration, Threatens Action
articles.summary: Election officials have warned against unlawful practices during ongoing voter registration, citing reports of coercion and intimidation, and cautioning they may suspend voting in affected areas if violations persist. In a statement issued today April 2, 2026, the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) said it is carrying out a range of pre-election activities to […]
articles.context: The warning from the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) comes amid heightened tensions following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has left the country divided and distrustful of electoral processes. The 2021 elections were marred by violence and allegations of voter suppression, leading to international scrutiny. As Ethiopia prepares for its next elections, the NEBE's emphasis on fair voter registration is crucial to restoring public confidence and ensuring a legitimate democratic process.

articles.title: IMF Chief Appoints Mauritania’s Zeine Zeidane as African Department Director
articles.summary: Mauritania’s Zeine Zeidane has been appointed as Director of the African Department (AFR) at the International Monetary Fund (IMF). Zeidane will succeed Abebe Aemro Selassie, who will retire
articles.context: Zeine Zeidane's appointment comes at a critical time for the African continent, particularly as Ethiopia navigates the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which has strained its economy and international relations. The IMF's engagement is crucial for Ethiopia, especially following the 2021 agreement to cease hostilities and the subsequent need for economic recovery and reform. Zeidane's leadership may influence IMF policies that directly impact Ethiopia's financial stability and growth prospects.

articles.title: MIDROC, TFG Hospitality Sign Master Agreement for 10 Properties in Ethiopia
articles.summary: MIDROC Investment Group and The First Group Hospitality, a Dubai-headquartered hospitality management firm, signed a master agreement to develop and operate 10 properties across Ethiopia. The portfolio includes
articles.context: The signing of the master agreement comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, is actively seeking foreign investment to revitalize the economy and promote stability. MIDROC, owned by Ethiopian-Saudi billionaire Mohammed Al-Amoudi, plays a crucial role in this strategy, aiming to enhance the country's hospitality sector and attract tourism.

articles.title: Gov’t raises fuel prices by 16.6% as subsidy burden reaches 272 billion birr
articles.summary: Ethiopia has implemented a fresh round of fuel price increases, citing mounting fiscal pressure from global oil market disruptions and a rapidly growing subsidy burden. The Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration announced that, effective April 1, 2026, the price of white diesel has risen by 16.6%, marking the second major adjustment within a single […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's fuel price hike comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which has strained national resources since its outbreak in November 2020. The government’s subsidy burden, now at 272 billion birr, reflects the impact of global oil price fluctuations and domestic unrest, complicating recovery efforts following the peace agreement signed in November 2022. This adjustment is crucial as it signals the government's struggle to balance fiscal stability with public discontent.

articles.title: NBE, China’ Central Banks Discuss Advancing Monetary Cooperation
articles.summary: National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) says its Governor Eyob Tekalign met with People’s Bank of China (PBOC) governor Pan Gongsheng on Thursday to advance a monetary cooperation aimed
articles.context: The meeting between NBE Governor Eyob Tekalign and PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng comes amid Ethiopia's efforts to stabilize its economy following a prolonged civil conflict that began in 2020, which has strained financial resources. Strengthening ties with China is particularly significant as Ethiopia seeks to enhance trade and investment, especially after the signing of the China-Ethiopia Comprehensive Strategic Cooperative Partnership in 2021, aimed at bolstering economic collaboration.

articles.title: Sixteen Kenyans missing in Russia after army recruitment
articles.summary: More than 250 Kenyans have gone to fight for Russia in Ukraine, mostly willingly, Kenya's foreign minister says.
articles.context: The recruitment of Kenyans to fight for Russia in Ukraine comes amid heightened tensions following Russia's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. This situation reflects a broader trend of foreign fighters joining the conflict, with Kenya's foreign minister, Alfred Mutua, acknowledging the voluntary nature of this enlistment. The Kenyan government is now grappling with the implications of its citizens engaging in a foreign war, raising concerns about national security and diplomatic relations with Western nations.

articles.title: ‘teleSign’: Ethio telecom Rolls out Digital Signature Platform
articles.summary: Ethio Telecom has launched a national digital signature platform, teleSign, in a move that amed at enabling secure online identity verification and expanding access to government services without
articles.context: Ethiopia has been navigating a complex political landscape since the outbreak of conflict in the Tigray region in November 2020, which has strained government services and digital infrastructure. The launch of teleSign comes as part of Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's broader digital transformation agenda, aimed at enhancing governance and rebuilding trust in public institutions amidst ongoing tensions and calls for greater accountability and transparency in the wake of the conflict.

articles.title: Ethiopia, China ink deal for RMB trade settlements
articles.summary: To strengthen Ethiopia’s financial resilience and further enhance economic ties with China—the country’s leading trade partner—the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) has unveiled a new initiative aimed at significantly increasing its foreign exchange reserves of the Chinese Yuan (RMB). This announcement was made at the conclusion of a high-level bilateral meeting between the Governor of […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been navigating economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, leading to significant disruptions in trade and investment. As Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy, strengthening ties with China—its largest trading partner—through RMB trade settlements is crucial. This initiative aligns with China's Belt and Road Initiative, which aims to enhance infrastructure and trade connectivity in the region, further solidifying Ethiopia's role in this strategic partnership.

articles.title: "كنت أسمع أصواتهم وهم يحترقون".. شهادات من سكان مدينة الدلنج السودانية
articles.summary: تتعرض الدلنج لقصف مدفعي متكرر، وسط اتهامات لقوات الدعم السريع المتحالفة مع الحركة الشعبية لتحرير السودان – شمال باستهداف الأحياء السكنية
articles.context: تعيش مدينة الدلنج في ولاية جنوب كردفان السودانية حالة من التوتر المتزايد منذ اندلاع النزاع بين قوات الدعم السريع والحركة الشعبية لتحرير السودان – شمال في عام 2011. تصاعدت حدة القصف المدفعي في الأسابيع الأخيرة، مما أدى إلى نزوح السكان وارتفاع عدد الضحايا، في ظل غياب أي اتفاقية سلام فعالة منذ اتفاقية أديس أبابا في 2011. هذه الأحداث تأتي في وقت حساس حيث تتزايد الضغوط الدولية لحل النزاع.

articles.title: ​NEBE Warns of Election Cancellations Over Voter Registration Coercion
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) has issued a strong warning regarding legal violations observed during the 7th general election voter registration process, stating it may cancel elections in specific constituencies where undue pressure is reported. The Board noted that while it is working to ensure a free, fair, and credible election, it has […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's political landscape has been tumultuous since the 2018 reforms led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, which aimed to democratize the country but also intensified ethnic tensions. The Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, has further complicated the electoral process, leading to concerns about voter coercion and manipulation. The upcoming 7th general elections, scheduled for 2025, are critical for stabilizing the nation amidst ongoing unrest and the need for credible governance.

articles.title: Somalia registers ship under national flag after over 3 decades
articles.summary: Registration represents 'the revival of Somalia’s maritime authority and the renewed credibility of the Somali flag,' says ports and marine transport minister
articles.context: For over three decades, Somalia has struggled with political instability and civil conflict, which severely impacted its maritime industry. The collapse of the central government in 1991 led to piracy and illegal fishing off its coast, undermining the country's maritime authority. The recent registration of a ship under the Somali flag marks a significant step towards rebuilding national governance and restoring international confidence in Somalia's maritime capabilities, particularly following the 2020 Somali Maritime Code's implementation.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Resumes Flights to Middle East Destinations
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa’s largest flight carrier, has resumed flights to some destinations in the Middle East after weeks of disruption caused by the conflict in the region. Mesfin
articles.context: The resumption of Ethiopian Airlines flights to the Middle East comes after a period of heightened tensions following the outbreak of conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region in November 2020, which has since expanded to involve various ethnic groups and regional actors. The African Union's peace talks, initiated in October 2022, aimed to stabilize the situation, but ongoing violence and political instability have continued to disrupt air travel and economic ties with Middle Eastern countries.

articles.title: DR Congo Book First World Cup Spot in 52-year with Victory over Jamaica
articles.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo have qualified for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after beating Jamaica 1-0 in their intercontinental play-off final in Mexico. The historic victory on
articles.context: The Democratic Republic of Congo's qualification for the 2026 FIFA World Cup comes amid ongoing political instability and conflict in the eastern regions, where armed groups have long contested control. This achievement is particularly significant as it represents a unifying moment for a nation grappling with the aftermath of the 1998-2003 Congo Wars and ongoing tensions, including the recent peace agreements aimed at stabilizing the region, such as the Nairobi Process initiated in 2022.

articles.title: Ethiopia Advances to AfCON 2027 Qualifying Round
articles.summary: Ethiopia sealed progress to the next stage of the Africa Cup of Nations PAMOJA 2027 qualifiers, beating Sao Tome and Principe 4-0 on aggregate in their preliminary round
articles.context: Ethiopia's advancement in the AfCON 2027 qualifiers comes amid ongoing political tensions and ethnic conflicts within the country, particularly following the Tigray War that officially ended in November 2022. The national team's success serves as a unifying force, promoting national pride and stability in a time of uncertainty. Additionally, Ethiopia's participation in the tournament reflects its efforts to strengthen regional ties within the East African Community, fostering cooperation through sports.

articles.title: Bank of China, AfDB review financing framework for Bishoftu Airport
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Airlines Group and the Ministry of Finance have held high-level talks with the Bank of China over financing for the planned Bishoftu International Airport, a $12.5 billion project expected to become Africa’s largest aviation hub. The April 1, 2026 meeting comes as Ethiopia steps up efforts to secure funding for the mega-project, while […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's push for the Bishoftu International Airport comes amid a backdrop of economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government is prioritizing infrastructure development to attract investment and stimulate growth, especially as it seeks to enhance its regional connectivity and position itself as a key player in East Africa's aviation sector. The involvement of the Bank of China and AfDB underscores the importance of international partnerships in this endeavor.

articles.title: Eritrea advance after 19-year absence from Afcon qualifying
articles.summary: Eritrea are one of six nations to reach the group stage of qualification for Afcon 2027, having played their first games in the competition since 2007.
articles.context: Eritrea's return to the Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) qualifiers comes after a prolonged absence due to internal political strife and a lack of investment in sports since the end of the Eritrean-Ethiopian War in 2000. The nation’s last participation in the qualifiers was in 2007, and their resurgence is seen as a potential unifying force amid ongoing tensions with Ethiopia, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict and the subsequent peace agreement.

articles.title: Container Shortage Reported Due to Maritime Transport Disruptions
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Maritime Authority (EMA) has announced a shortage in the supply of empty containers for Ethiopia’s export products, citing disruptions in maritime transport caused by the security crisis in the Middle East. In an official letter sent to the Ethiopian Customs Commission, the Authority indicated that container supplies have become inconsistent because several shipping […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which escalated in November 2020, has strained Ethiopia's logistics and trade capabilities. Additionally, the recent escalation of tensions in the Middle East, particularly following the Israel-Palestine conflict resurgence in October 2023, has disrupted global shipping routes, exacerbating container shortages. This situation is critical for Ethiopia, as it relies heavily on maritime transport for its export-driven economy, particularly in sectors like coffee and textiles.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s MPC Holds Off on Lifting Credit Cap, Citing Global Uncertainty
articles.summary: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) concluded its latest meeting on Tuesday without issuing a concrete decision on the much-anticipated lifting of the credit cap, opting instead to maintain current policy settings amid rising global economic uncertainty.In a statement released following the March 31 session, the MPC indicated that […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges due to the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, leading to widespread displacement and disruption. The government's efforts to stabilize the economy include the implementation of a credit cap to control inflation and manage liquidity. As global economic conditions remain volatile, the MPC's decision to maintain the credit cap reflects ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures and the need for cautious monetary policy amidst recovery efforts.

articles.title: Failed Fuel Delivery Prompts New Allocation Rules, Crackdown on Underground Trade
articles.summary: The federal government has introduced sweeping fuel prioritization measures and intensified a crackdown on illegal trading after a delivery of more than 180,000 metric tons of fuel failed to arrive, heightening concerns surrounding supply disruptions linked to conflict in the Middle East. The shortfall, affecting shipments already in the procurement pipeline, comes as global fuel […]
articles.context: The recent failure of the fuel delivery is compounded by Ethiopia's ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which has strained logistics and supply chains since the civil war began in November 2020. Additionally, the Ethiopian government has been grappling with rising fuel prices and inflation, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly following the 2023 Israel-Hamas conflict that has disrupted global oil supplies.

articles.title: Over 180,000 Metric Tons of Fuel Failed to Arrive Due to Conflict
articles.summary: Minister of Trade and Regional Integration (MoTRI) , Kassahun Gofe announced that over 180,000 metric tons of fuel, which Ethiopia intended to import, has failed to reach the country due to the war and instability in the Middle East. The Minister disclosed this during a briefing regarding new government decisions aimed at mitigating the impact […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflict since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, which has exacerbated regional instability and disrupted supply chains. The ongoing tensions in the Middle East, particularly due to conflicts involving key oil-producing nations, have further complicated Ethiopia's fuel imports. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been striving to stabilize the economy while navigating these multifaceted challenges, including the recent peace agreement with Tigray forces in November 2022.

articles.title: More than 70 miners killed in South Sudan as government and opposition trade blame
articles.summary: Both government and opposition groups have been accused of being responsible for the deadly attack.
articles.context: The deadly attack on miners in South Sudan occurs against the backdrop of ongoing tensions between the government, led by President Salva Kiir, and opposition forces loyal to Riek Machar, stemming from a civil war that erupted in 2013. Despite the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending hostilities, sporadic violence continues, particularly in resource-rich areas, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis and complicating the fragile political landscape.

articles.title: Commercial flights resume to Somalia's South West state after nearly 2-week halt
articles.summary: South West state had suspended flights over dispute with Mogadishu on state president Laftagareen's mandate
articles.context: The suspension of flights to Somalia's South West state stemmed from a political dispute between the regional government, led by President Abdiaziz Laftagareen, and the federal government in Mogadishu regarding the legitimacy of Laftagareen's mandate, which has been contested since his election in 2018. This conflict highlights ongoing tensions between regional authorities and the central government, particularly following the controversial 2021 elections that exacerbated divisions within Somali politics.

articles.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
articles.summary: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, exacerbated by the 2021 military coup led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, has created a power vacuum and intensified violence, particularly in regions like Darfur. The collapse of civil order and the resurgence of armed groups have led to widespread human rights abuses, including sexual violence, as reported by organizations like Amnesty International. This situation is further complicated by the lack of accountability and the international community's limited response.

articles.title: AU Extends Condolence after Passing of former Algerian President Zeroual
articles.summary: African Union Commission Chairperson Mahmoud Ali Youssouf has extended his heartfelt condolences over the passing former Algerian President Liamine Zeroual on Sunday. Former President Zéroual died at the
articles.context: Liamine Zeroual served as Algeria's president from 1994 to 1999 during a tumultuous period marked by the Algerian Civil War, which claimed over 200,000 lives. His leadership was pivotal in initiating the "Civil Concord" policy in 1999, aimed at reconciling the nation post-conflict. His passing comes at a time when Algeria is navigating regional tensions, particularly concerning its relationships with neighboring countries and the ongoing instability in the Sahel region.

articles.title: Korir banned for five years over doping admission
articles.summary: Former New York marathon winner Albert Korir of Kenya is banned for five years after admitting to doping.
articles.context: Albert Korir's five-year ban comes amid heightened scrutiny of doping in athletics, particularly in Kenya, which has faced a surge in doping cases among its elite runners. The Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU) has been actively enforcing anti-doping regulations since the 2016 Rio Olympics, where several Kenyan athletes were implicated. This ban underscores ongoing efforts to restore integrity in a country renowned for its long-distance running prowess, following the suspension of over 50 athletes in recent years.

articles.title: African Union urges peaceful dialogue in Somalia amid federal government, South West state tensions
articles.summary: Bloc ‘stands ready to support dialogue and reconciliation,’ says statement
articles.context: Tensions between Somalia's federal government and the South West state have escalated following the controversial dismissal of the state’s president, Abdiaziz Lafta Gareen, in July 2023. This political strife has raised concerns about stability in the region, especially as Somalia prepares for its upcoming elections, initially scheduled for late 2023. The African Union's call for dialogue comes amid fears that unresolved conflicts could exacerbate security challenges posed by the Al-Shabaab insurgency.

articles.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
articles.summary: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has seen a surge in wildlife trafficking, exacerbated by the 2019 ban on the export of certain animal species, which has driven smugglers to exploit niche markets like the collection of rare ants. The rise of social media platforms has further fueled this trend, allowing traffickers to reach global collectors. This phenomenon reflects broader challenges in wildlife conservation and enforcement efforts amid ongoing political instability and corruption in the region.

articles.title: Abel Scores Twice as Ethiopia Beat Sao Tome e Principe in AfCON 2027 Qualifying Opener
articles.summary: Ethiopia took major step forwards to the next stage of CAF Africa Cup of Nations PAMOJA 2027 qualifiers with a 3-0 win over Sao Tome e Principe in
articles.context: Ethiopia's victory over Sao Tome e Principe comes amidst a challenging backdrop of internal conflict and political instability, particularly following the Tigray War, which officially ended in November 2022. The Ethiopian national team’s success in the AFCON 2027 qualifiers is seen as a unifying moment for the country, fostering national pride and solidarity as it seeks to recover from recent turmoil and enhance its international standing in sports.

articles.title: NBE Launches Hunts for Consulting Firm to Design New Headquarters
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopia’s Central Bank has put a call out to consulting firms to undertake architectural and engineering design for a New Headquarter construction project. The National
articles.context: The National Bank of Ethiopia's initiative to construct a new headquarters comes amid ongoing economic challenges and a push for modernization in the wake of the 2020 Tigray conflict, which strained the country's financial systems. The government aims to bolster investor confidence and stabilize the economy, particularly as it seeks to implement the Homegrown Economic Reform agenda initiated in 2020. This project reflects Ethiopia's broader efforts to enhance institutional infrastructure and attract foreign investment.

articles.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
articles.summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.
articles.context: The rise of social media in Kenya and Ghana has led to increased vulnerability for women, particularly in the context of online exploitation. In recent years, incidents of cyber harassment have surged, coinciding with a broader global conversation about digital privacy and consent. The involvement of foreign actors, such as the Russian man in this case, highlights the transnational nature of online abuse, raising concerns about the effectiveness of local laws and protections for victims.

articles.title: Somali forces kill over 42 al-Shabaab terrorists, including 2 ringleaders, in military operations
articles.summary: Abdihamid Ali Abdullahi Macalin, Suleyman Omar Fidow, among al-Qaeda-linked terror group's members, killed in military operations in Middle Juba and Lower Shabelle regions, says official statement
articles.context: The recent military operations in Somalia come amid heightened efforts by the Somali National Army to combat al-Shabaab, particularly following the 2020 agreement between the Somali government and regional states to intensify counter-terrorism efforts. The killing of key ringleaders Abdihamid Ali Abdullahi Macalin and Suleyman Omar Fidow is significant as it disrupts al-Shabaab's operational capabilities and reflects ongoing U.S. support for Somali forces in their fight against the group, which has escalated since the withdrawal of AMISOM troops in 2021.

articles.title: Libya says drifting Russian tanker moved to international waters off coast
articles.summary: National Oil Corporation tasked with safely unloading vessel after operation
articles.context: The drifting Russian tanker off Libya's coast comes amid heightened tensions in the Eastern Mediterranean, where energy exploration has intensified following the 2019 Libyan ceasefire. The National Oil Corporation's involvement reflects ongoing challenges in securing oil resources amidst the power struggle between the Government of National Unity and rival factions. Additionally, the situation is complicated by Russia's increasing influence in the region, particularly through military and economic ties established since the Libyan conflict escalated in 2011.

articles.title: UNGA Adopts Resolution Declaring Slave Trade as “Gravest Crime against Humanity”
articles.summary: UNITED NATIONS – The UN General Assembly (UNGA) on Wednesday adopted a draft resolution of “Declaration of the Trafficking of Enslaved Africans and Racialized Chattel Enslavement of Africans
articles.context: The adoption of the UNGA resolution comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to address historical injustices related to slavery and human trafficking, particularly in the context of the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The resolution aligns with Ethiopia's commitment to the African Union's Agenda 2063, which emphasizes the importance of human rights and dignity. Additionally, it reflects a growing international focus on reparative justice for the descendants of enslaved Africans, particularly in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Tanzania’s Former President Named AU Envoy for the Horn of Africa, Red Sea
articles.summary: The Chairperson of the African Union Commission Chair Mahmoud Ali Youssouf, has appointed Tanzania’s former president Jakaya Mrisho Kikwete as High Representative for the Horn of Africa and
articles.context: Jakaya Kikwete's appointment comes at a critical time as the Horn of Africa grapples with ongoing conflicts, including the Tigray War in Ethiopia, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. His role is significant as regional tensions persist, particularly involving Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Somalia, and as the African Union seeks to bolster diplomatic efforts to stabilize the region amid humanitarian crises and political unrest.

articles.title: Ethiopian Signs Lease Deal with AerCap for two Boeing 777-300ERSFs
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopian Airlines has signed lease agreements with AerCap Holdings N.V. for two Boeing 777-300ERSF converted aircrafts. The aircraft, also known as “The Big Twin,” will
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa's largest airline, is expanding its fleet amidst ongoing regional tensions, particularly with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) conflict that has impacted the economy since late 2020. The lease of the Boeing 777-300ERSFs comes as the airline seeks to enhance its cargo capabilities to support trade routes, especially in light of the recent peace agreement signed in November 2022, which aims to stabilize the region and boost economic recovery.

articles.title: Medical group accuses RSF of killing 16 civilians in Sudan’s Darfur
articles.summary: Group says victims were executed and buried in mass graves, calls for urgent international action
articles.context: The reported killings in Darfur come amid escalating violence following the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). This conflict has reignited long-standing ethnic tensions and instability in the region, which has faced atrocities since the Darfur genocide in the early 2000s. The international community, including the African Union and UN, has been urged to intervene as civilian casualties continue to rise.

articles.title: UN chief 'strongly' condemns deadly drone strike on hospital in Sudan, calls for ceasefire
articles.summary: Guterres calls on parties to 'immediately' de-escalate fighting and agree on cessation of hostilities, his spokesman says
articles.context: The recent drone strike on a hospital in Sudan occurs amid escalating violence between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, which began in April 2023. This conflict has resulted in widespread humanitarian crises, with over 5 million people displaced. The UN-brokered ceasefire agreements, including the Jeddah process initiated in May 2023, have repeatedly failed, highlighting the urgent need for international intervention and a sustainable resolution to the ongoing violence.

articles.title: ‘Dine for Generations’: PM Abiy Inaugurates Niin Lee Palm Lodge in Afar Region
articles.summary: Niin Lee Palm Lodge, a new tourist destination developed through the Dine for Generations initiative, has been officially opened in the Afar region. The inauguration on Sunday comes
articles.context: The inauguration of Niin Lee Palm Lodge is significant amid ongoing efforts to revitalize Ethiopia's tourism sector following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which severely impacted the economy and regional stability. The Dine for Generations initiative aims to promote sustainable development and foster peace in the Afar region, which has historically faced challenges from both internal conflicts and climate change. This development aligns with the Ethiopian government's broader strategy to enhance tourism as a means of economic recovery.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Inflation Rate Stands at 9.7% in February
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for February rose at 9.7% rate from the same time a year ago, according to the Ethiopian Statistics Services (ESS)
articles.context: Ethiopia's inflation rate has been a growing concern amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The conflict disrupted supply chains and agricultural production, contributing to rising prices. Additionally, the government’s efforts to stabilize the economy through reforms and international support are critical as the nation seeks recovery from the economic fallout of the civil war and the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: Why acknowledging slavery is not enough without reparatory justice
articles.summary: Ghana urges the UN to recognize the transatlantic slave trade as a crime against humanity and pursue reparatory justice.

articles.summary: Over 1,000 Kenyans and thousands of Africans are being drawn into Russia’s war in Ukraine, with many killed, missing, or trapped.

articles.summary: بورتسودان 20 مارس 2026 – عيدٌ آخر والسودانيون يفقدون الأحبة، والأهل تائهون في مواقع النزوح واللجوء، بيننا تقترب الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع من عامها الرابع، تلك الذكرى التي ستحل بين عيدين. ومع استمرار القتال وتحليق المسيرات في أنحاء البلاد، وما تخلفه من أوضاع كارثية، يحل عيد الفطر على السودانيين بين نازح ولاجئ وعائدين بلا شيء. وأشار العديد من السودانيين على حساباتهم في وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي إلى اتساع رقعة المأساة التي رسمتها حرب 15 أبريل 2023 مع حلول عيد الفطر اليوم الجمعة. وقالت الباحثة الاجتماعية د.

articles.title: نيكولاس “فينك” هايسوم.. رحيل إنسان حقيقي ومناضل أفريقي وحدوي
articles.summary: ياسر عرمان لطالما أبهرني الجنوب أفريقيون البيض الذين امتلكوا الرؤية والشجاعة للوقوف ضد نظام الفصل العنصري والنضال من أجل مجتمع لا عنصري. وعلى وجه الخصوص، المحامون العظماء والقادة التقدميون من البيض، مثل برام فيشر، وجورج بيزوس، ونيكولاس “فينك” هايسوم. كان فينك إنسانًا حقيقيًا ووحدويًا أفريقيًا ملتزمًا وقف بثبات ضد نظام الفصل العنصري. ومن بين صفاته العديدة دعك عن المناصب التي شغلها، بما فيها كونه مستشارًا للرئيس نيلسون مانديلا، ومساهمته في العديد من عمليات السلام مثل عملية السلام في نيفاشا وعملية تاميل تايغر في سيرلانكا، فقد ركّز على قضايا الإصلاح والحكم الديمقراطي والمصالحة وقضايا العدالة.

articles.summary: محمد الامين عبد النبي تشهد الساحة السياسية والعسكرية في السودان مرحلة شديدة التعقيد، تتداخل فيها الحسابات العسكرية مع الصراعات السياسية، في ظل تصاعد التوتر بين قيادة الجيش السوداني والحركة الإسلامية. هذا التوتر الذي ظل لسنوات طويلة مستتراً خلف تحالفات الضرورة وتقاطعات المصالح، بدأ في الآونة الأخيرة يخرج إلى العلن تدريجياً، خاصة بعد تصنيف الحركة الإسلامية كمنظمة إرهابية، وهي خطوة أعادت ترتيب خطوط التماس بين الطرفين وأطلقت ديناميات صراع جديدة داخل معسكري الجيش والإسلاميين على حد سواء. لم يكن التحالف بين المؤسسة العسكرية والحركة الإسلامية أمراً طارئاً في تاريخ السودان الحديث. فمنذ انقلاب الانقاذ الذي أوصل نظام الإسلاميين إلى السلطة، تشكلت...

articles.title: السودان يستعد لاحتمالات شح وغلاء الأدوية مع استمرار الحرب على إيران
articles.summary: الخرطوم 20 مارس 2026 – تثير الحرب على إيران الدائرة منذ نحو ثلاثة أسابيع مخاوف من انعكاسات خطيرة على إمدادات الأدوية وارتفاع تكاليف التصنيع والاستيراد، وسط توقعات بتفاقم آثارها على السودان ما اضطر السلطات للاستعداد لأسواء الاحتمالات. ويعاني السودان أصلا من تدمير قطاع التصنيع الدوائي وشح النقد الأجنبي. وتضرر نحو 85% من القطاع الصناعي في السودان جراء الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع منذ 2023، بما في ذلك مصانع الأدوية بولاية الخرطوم، التي كانت تنتج نحو 80 صنفا دوائيا، حيث تعرض بعضها للتدمير، بينما توقف إنتاج البعض الآخر بسبب انعدام الأمن، وفقًا لإحصاءات رسمية. وعقد وزير الصحة السوداني هيثم محمد إبراهيم، الأسبوع الماضي، الاجتماع...

articles.title: قائد الدعم السريع يجدد القبول بهدنة إنسانية «جادة»
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 20 مارس 2026 – جدد قائد قوات الدعم السريع محمد حمدان دقلو حميدتي، الجمعة، موافقته الصريحة على هدنة جادة وذات مصداقية للأغراض الإنسانية الخالصة. وقال حميدتي في كلمة له بمناسبة عيد الفطر إن الهدنة الإنسانية يجب أن تكفل وصول المساعدات إلى المدنيين بلا عوائق، وفتح الممرات الآمنة وتخفيف معاناة المتضررين وحماية النازحين واللاجئين. وأضاف قائد الدعم السريع أن تنفيذ الهدنة يجب ان يضمن عدم استغلالها لمآرب عسكرية أو سياسية ضيقة – حسب تعبيره -. وكان قائد الجيش السوداني عبد الفتاح البرهان قد جدد أمس رفضه لأي هدنة أو وقف إطلاق نار قبل أن تتجمع قوات الدعم السريع في مناطق بعينها وتسلم أسلحتها.

articles.title: Troika, aid agencies raise alarm over escalating violence in South Sudan
articles.summary: Aid agencies and Western diplomats warn of a worsening humanitarian crisis in South Sudan’s Jonglei region as conflict escalates.

articles.title: Rwanda church strips 35 pastors over genocide convictions

articles.title: Kigali beats Nairobi in battle of financial hubs
articles.summary: Kigali is outpacing Nairobi as East Africa’s financial hub, leveraging aggressive tax incentives and regulatory efficiency to attract global capital, while Kenya scrambles to close the gap.

articles.title: مسيرات الدعم السريع تهاجم الدبة بشمال السودان فجر عيد الفطر
articles.summary: الدبة، 20 مارس 2026 – هاجمت مسيرات استراتيجية تابعة لقوات الدعم السريع، الجمعة، مدينة الدبة شمالي السودان مما أدى لانقطاع التيار الكهربائي إثر غارات استهدفت محيط محطة كهرباء المدينة. وقال المدير التنفيذي لمحلية الدبة بالولاية الشمالية ورئيس اللجنة الأمنية بالمحلية محمد صابر إن قوات الدعم السريع استهدفت صباح الجمعة وفي أول أيام عيد الفطر مدينة الدبة بعدد من المسيرات الاستراتيجية. وأكد المدير التنفيذي للمحلية في تصريح صحفي أن المسيرات استهدفت عددا من الأعيان المدنية وكلية الهندسة مما أدى إلى إصابة ثلاثة أشخاص بجروح. وأوضح أن قوات الدعم السريع كعادتها تستهدف الأعيان المدنية لزرع الهلع والخوف في قلوب المواطنين العزل بالدبة أول أيام عيد الفطر.

articles.title: Mombasa, Lamu dock more ships as Dar lags
articles.summary: Gulf conflict reroutes thousands of vehicles to Mombasa and Lamu, straining ports and threatening East African exports.

articles.title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
articles.summary: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.
articles.context: Kenya is currently experiencing its worst drought in over four decades, exacerbated by climate change and the ongoing conflict in neighboring Ethiopia, which has disrupted food supplies and trade routes. The 2020 cessation of hostilities agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray forces has not fully stabilized the region, leaving millions vulnerable. As of October 2023, Oxfam reports that 26 million people across the Horn of Africa are facing extreme hunger, highlighting the urgent need for humanitarian assistance.

articles.title: السلطات التشادية تبدأ حملة قمع أمنية واسعة في الحدود مع السودان

articles.title: البرهان يجدد شروطه للقبول بأي مبادرة سلام مع «الدعم السريع»
articles.summary: الخرطوم 19 مارس 2026 – قال قائد الجيش السوداني ورئيس مجلس السيادة عبد الفتاح البرهان، الخميس، إنه يمكن التعاطي مع أي مبادرة سلام مع قوات الدعم السريع وفقا لعدة شروط. وأكد البرهان في خطاب وجهه للسودانيين بمناسبة عيد الفطر ليل الخميس، أن قيادة الدولة حريصةً على التعاطي الإيجابي والمسؤول مع كل مبادرةٍ صادقة لسلام، لكن بما يحقق الأمن ولا يُعيد إنتاج الحرب ولا يُبقي على أسبابها أو أدواتها. وأضاف البرهان أن على الوسطاء والساعون إلى إنهاء الحرب النهوض بمسؤولياتهم والمساعدة في إنفاذ مبادرات السلام التي قدّمتها الحكومة السودانية. وتابع قائلا: “إن مبادراتٌ الحكومة تنطلق من إرادة السودانيين وتستجيب لمطلبهم العادل في تفكيك وإنهاء أي دورٍ سياسي أو...

articles.title: مسيّرات الجيش تقطع «شريان» الإمداد وتغير قواعد المعارك في كردفان

articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.title: السلطات السودانية تعتقل القيادي الإسلامي الناجي عبد الله
articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – اعتقلت السلطات في السودان، الأحد، القيادي الإسلامي البارز الناجي عبد الله، وفقاً لما أفاد به أحد قادة المقاومة الشعبية. وظل الناجي عبد الله وهو أحد قيادات المقاومة الشعبية التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش في حربه ضد قوات الدعم السريع يجاهر بمواقفه المؤيدة للقضية الفلسطينية وداعماً لحركة المقاومة الإسلامية “حماس”. وأعلن الناجي في الثالث من مارس الجاري، خلال مخاطبته إفطاراً رمضانياً أمام حشد من مجموعات عسكرية محسوبة على التيار الإسلامي، دعمهم لإيران في الحرب التي تقودها ضد إسرائيل والولايات المتحدة الأميركية. وأثارت تلك التصريحات ردود فعل واسعة حيث أعلن القائد العام للجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان تبرؤه من تلك المجموعة، مؤكداً...

articles.title: «كبر» يكذب صحة مقطع فيديو يتحدث عن «البرهان» أثار جدلًا واسعًا
articles.summary: الخرطوم 16 مارس 2026 – نفى رئيس شورى المؤتمر الوطني – المحلول، والي شمال دارفور الأسبق عثمان محمد يوسف كبر، فجر الاثنين، صحة مقطع فيديو أثار جدلًا واسعًا في السودان تحدث خلاله عن رئيس مجلس السيادة وقائد الجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان. ونشرت قناة “اسكاي نيوز عربية” الإماراتية، الأحد، تقريرًا حوى مقطع فيديو يظهر كبر جالسًا على كرسي مريح يتحدث إلى أشخاص قربه في جلسة يبدو أنها غير رسمية، دون أن تُظهر اللقطات هؤلاء الأشخاص الجالسين قربه. وقال كبر، في مقطع الفيديو، إنهم “تحدثوا للبرهان لتسلميه المجلس العسكري لسحق المتظاهرين وتسليم السلطة إلى الإسلاميين، وهو لم يكن له دور، وما كان محسوبًا ضابطًا، حيث كان يجلس مثل الحرس خارج الباب”. وقال كبر، في بيان،...

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.

articles.title: Inside Ethiopia's 'smart' police station
articles.summary: Ethiopia unveils its first unmanned, 'smart' police station where citizens can report crimes on automated touch screens.
articles.context: Ethiopia's introduction of its first unmanned 'smart' police station comes amid ongoing tensions following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and has left deep societal scars. As the government seeks to modernize its security infrastructure and regain public trust, this initiative reflects a broader push for technological integration in governance, particularly in urban areas like Addis Ababa, where security concerns remain heightened.
